CRML.headline: Critical Metals to Acquire European Lithium
CRML.description: Critical Metals Corp. (NASDAQ: CRML) announced a proposed acquisition of European Lithium Ltd. (ASX: EUR) for approximately US$835 million, with European Lithium shareholders receiving 0.035 Critical Metals shares per share held. The transaction aims to consolidate 100% ownership of the Tanbreez rare earth project in Greenland, strengthen Critical Metals' balance sheet with ~US$219 million in cash, and reduce shareholder dilution by canceling European Lithium's 34% stake in Critical Metals. Comp

XE.headline: Cathie Wood Bets Big On New IPO: Buys Shares Of Amazon-Backed X-Energy To Expand Nuclear Investments
XE.description: Ark Invest, led by Cathie Wood, purchased shares of X-Energy Inc (XE) across multiple ETFs following the company's IPO on Friday. X-Energy, backed by Amazon, priced shares at $23 and opened at $30.11, closing at $29.20. The stock surged 14.3% to $33.38 on Monday. This investment reflects Ark's growing focus on nuclear energy as a disruptive technology, driven by AI data center demand and the Trump administration's support for the sector.

XE.headline: Fluor Is Having a Big Year. Is Now Still a Good Time to Buy the Stock?
XE.description: Fluor, an engineering and construction company, is experiencing strong performance in 2026 driven by growing demand in nuclear energy and data center projects. The company recently secured contracts with Terawulf and X-energy and maintains a $4.6 billion energy backlog. However, the nuclear market is expected to grow modestly from $40.4 billion in 2025 to $52.6 billion by 2034, suggesting investors should build positions gradually rather than rush in, as long-term gains will likely be more subst

OGN.headline: Stock Market Today, April 27: Organon Shares Surge After Sun Pharma Agrees to Acquire Company in All Cash Deal
OGN.description: Organon & Co. shares surged 16.87% to $13.16 after Sun Pharmaceutical Industries agreed to acquire the women's health company in an all-cash deal valued at $14 per share. The acquisition, expected to close in early 2027, comes as relief for Organon, which has struggled since its 2021 spinoff from Merck with $8 billion in long-term debt and declining profitability.

OGN.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Futures Fall, S&P 500 Gains As Trump Halts Iran Talks— Organon, SAP, Cheetah Net Supply Chain Service In Focus (UPDATED)
OGN.description: U.S. stock futures fell on Monday as President Trump canceled Iran talks, citing divisions within Tehran. The market awaits the Federal Reserve's Wednesday meeting with 100% likelihood of unchanged rates. Key movers include Organon jumping 15% on a $11.75B acquisition deal, SAP rising 6.14% on cloud adoption acceleration, and Cheetah Net plummeting 22.12% following a reverse stock split announcement.

USAR.headline: USA Rare Earth Just Made a $2.8 Billion Strategic Acquisition That Sent the Stock Soaring. Time to Buy?
USAR.description: USA Rare Earth acquired Serra Verde Group for $2.8 billion to expand its rare-earth mining and processing capabilities, gaining control of Brazil's Pela Ema mine. The acquisition accelerates the company's timeline to profitability and supports U.S. efforts to reduce dependence on China for critical minerals. While the move is strategically sound, investors should be cautious due to execution risks.

SMR.headline: NuScale Power Stock Is Cheap, but Does That Make It a Buy Now?
SMR.description: NuScale Power's stock has fallen 75% from its 52-week high, making it appear cheap. However, the company remains a risky startup that has yet to secure its first customer for its small modular nuclear reactor (SMR) technology. While the company has regulatory approval, government support, and pre-ordered parts for up to 12 reactors, investors should wait until NuScale completes its first actual SMR sale before investing, as the technology remains unproven in commercial deployment.

RKLB.headline: Are These 5 Stocks Your Best Buys If the Market Crashes Again?
RKLB.description: Neil Rozenbaum identifies five stocks he would buy immediately if the market experiences another downturn: Nebius, AMD, SoFi, Meta, and Rocket Lab. The article also mentions Anthropic's new design tool as a potential disruptor in the SaaS space.

INTC.headline: Here's What I Think Is Going On With Nvidia Stock
INTC.description: Nvidia stock declined over 6% in Q1 2026 due to AI stock valuation concerns and geopolitical tensions, but has recently rebounded to a new all-time high, reaching a $5 trillion market cap. The recovery is driven by strong AI demand signals from TSMC and Intel, expectations of another blowout earnings report in May, and an attractive valuation of 24x forward earnings compared to previous highs of 48x, suggesting sustained positive momentum ahead.

INTC.headline: Why May Could Be a Turning Point for Palantir Stock
INTC.description: Palantir Technologies has declined 19% this year due to valuation concerns and AI demand uncertainty, but may be reaching a turning point in May. The company's fundamentals remain strong with triple-digit commercial revenue growth, record contract values, and expanding customer base. With valuation now more reasonable at 108x forward earnings (down from 240x peak) and broader AI sector recovery underway, Wall Street expects 30% upside over the next 12 months.

INTC.headline: This Semiconductor Stock Is Up 36% in 2026, and It's Not Too Late to Buy Right Now
INTC.description: ASML Holding, the leading manufacturer of semiconductor lithography equipment, has surged 36% in 2026 driven by booming demand from major chipmakers. The company exceeded Q1 revenue expectations with 13% year-over-year growth and raised full-year 2026 guidance to 16% growth. Major orders from SK Hynix, Samsung, and anticipated large purchases from TSMC and Micron signal strong continued demand for both advanced AI chips and memory chips, positioning ASML to potentially exceed its 2030 revenue ta

INTC.headline: With Intel, AMD, Nvidia, and More Rising Sharply Last Week, Have Chip Stocks Become Overvalued?
INTC.description: Chip stocks surged last week following strong earnings from Intel and Texas Instruments, confirming robust AI demand beyond just GPUs. However, the author argues that while the AI boom remains strong, chip stocks have become overvalued in aggregate, with forward P/E ratios based on extremely elevated earnings trajectories and potential supply chain constraints ahead. The author recommends staying on the sidelines at current valuations.

INTC.headline: S&P 500 Holds Near Highs Despite Oil Shock and Weak Breadth Data
INTC.description: U.S. equity markets held near record highs despite geopolitical tensions in the Middle East driving oil prices above $100/barrel. The collapse of U.S.-Iran negotiations triggered an energy shock, while major tech developments included OpenAI's partnership with Qualcomm on smartphone processors, Microsoft's loss of exclusive OpenAI access, and China's forced unwinding of Meta's AI acquisition. Consumer weakness emerged with Domino's cutting guidance, while positive credit metrics in auto lending

INTC.headline: Why Arm Holdings Stock Was Tumbling Today
INTC.description: Arm Holdings stock pulled back after surging on Intel's strong earnings report, as investors took profits following a 50% rally in three weeks. Despite the pullback, the CPU market remains buoyed by growing demand from Agentic AI. Arm's valuation has stretched to a P/E of 130, though its new chip design and $25 billion revenue target by 2031 offer growth potential.

INTC.headline: Q1 Earnings Kick Off: Strong Results and Record CEO Confidence Anchor the Market
INTC.description: Q1 2026 earnings season shows strong corporate performance with the S&P 500 projected to deliver 13.2% earnings growth, its sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit growth. Big banks reported solid results with investment banking rebounds, while semiconductor leaders TSMC and ASML posted record revenues driven by AI infrastructure demand. CEO confidence remains high with the Late Earnings Report Index at a record low of 55, indicating management visibility and economic resilience despite geopol

INTC.headline: S&P 500 Near Highs as Earnings and Fed Outlook Limit Further Gains
INTC.description: US stocks are modestly lower on Monday as investors weigh Middle East tensions and prepare for a busy week of Big Tech earnings. Five of the 'Magnificent Seven' companies report this week, with the Federal Reserve meeting also in focus. The S&P 500 has reached record highs around 7,175 but faces consolidation pressure from overbought conditions. Oil prices are rising toward $100/barrel amid supply concerns.

INTC.headline: The Key Metric Every Tesla Investor Is Focusing On
INTC.description: Tesla investors are increasingly focused on cash flow forecasts as the company commits to aggressive capital spending of $25 billion in 2026 to build six new factories and support projects like Megapack production and semiconductor fabrication. While Tesla is expected to burn cash through 2027, it should return to free cash flow generation in 2028. The investment thesis hinges on robotaxi and Optimus revenue materializing as planned, with any delays potentially impacting stock performance.

INTC.headline: Consumer Tech News (Apr 20-24): U.S. Warns China Over AI Theft, Big Tech Faces Political Scrutiny Over AI & More
INTC.description: The Trump administration warned China of stronger enforcement against AI technology theft and distillation of frontier models. Meanwhile, Big Tech faces political scrutiny over AI development, with Sen. Josh Hawley urging Republicans to reject contributions from pro-AI groups. Major tech companies reported mixed earnings, with Intel and Texas Instruments beating estimates, while Tesla sales fell 24.3% in California. Key developments include Meta collecting employee keystroke data for AI training

QBTS.headline: The First 5 Quantum Computing Stocks I'd Buy If I Were Starting From Scratch
QBTS.description: The article recommends five quantum computing stocks for investors: IonQ and D-Wave Quantum as pure-play quantum computing companies with high growth potential but higher risk, balanced with more conservative picks in Alphabet and Microsoft, which have significant resources and quantum breakthroughs. Nvidia is positioned to benefit from hybrid computing environments combining quantum and classical computers.

QBTS.headline: Top Quantum Computing Stocks to Buy in April
QBTS.description: The quantum computing market is expected to grow to $72 billion by 2035, with widespread availability potentially by 2030. The article recommends three stocks: IonQ for its trapped-ion technology and accuracy advantages, D-Wave Quantum for its quantum annealing applications in optimization problems, and Nvidia for bridging traditional and quantum computing through hybrid infrastructure solutions.

ASTS.headline: Is SpaceX Really Worth $1.75 Trillion -- or Is That Actually Too Low?
ASTS.description: SpaceX's upcoming IPO values the company at $1.75 trillion, which exceeds traditional valuation metrics but may be justified by its growth potential. While the P/S ratio of 95 appears expensive, the article argues this is irrelevant given SpaceX's rapid expansion prospects, particularly through Starlink's satellite internet services, the launch business, and emerging opportunities in space-based AI data centers and Starship commercialization. However, significant risks remain including competiti

TXN.headline: Stock Market Today, April 27: Qualcomm Rises on Reported OpenAI Smartphone Chip Collaboration
TXN.description: Qualcomm stock rose 0.95% to $150.26 on reports of a potential collaboration with OpenAI on AI-focused smartphone processors. The development highlights long-term growth potential in on-device AI, though commercial benefits are expected in future product cycles. Near-term performance remains dependent on smartphone demand and licensing revenue.

TXN.headline: With Intel, AMD, Nvidia, and More Rising Sharply Last Week, Have Chip Stocks Become Overvalued?
TXN.description: Chip stocks surged last week following strong earnings from Intel and Texas Instruments, confirming robust AI demand beyond just GPUs. However, the author argues that while the AI boom remains strong, chip stocks have become overvalued in aggregate, with forward P/E ratios based on extremely elevated earnings trajectories and potential supply chain constraints ahead. The author recommends staying on the sidelines at current valuations.

TXN.headline: Should You Buy SCHD at $31 or Wait for a Better Entry Point?
TXN.description: The Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD) is trading near its 52-week high at $31 per share, up over 20% in the past year. While the ETF offers a solid 3.4% dividend yield and holds quality dividend-paying companies, the article advises against trying to time the market. Instead, investors should consider dollar-cost averaging to build positions incrementally rather than waiting for a potentially lower entry point.

TXN.headline: Consumer Tech News (Apr 20-24): U.S. Warns China Over AI Theft, Big Tech Faces Political Scrutiny Over AI & More
TXN.description: The Trump administration warned China of stronger enforcement against AI technology theft and distillation of frontier models. Meanwhile, Big Tech faces political scrutiny over AI development, with Sen. Josh Hawley urging Republicans to reject contributions from pro-AI groups. Major tech companies reported mixed earnings, with Intel and Texas Instruments beating estimates, while Tesla sales fell 24.3% in California. Key developments include Meta collecting employee keystroke data for AI training

IREN.headline: What Is One of the Best AI Stocks to Buy Right Now?
IREN.description: IREN, a data center infrastructure company, is positioned as a strong AI stock investment due to booming demand for data center capacity driven by AI spending expected to reach $600 billion in 2026. The company has secured a $9.7 billion Microsoft deal and is on pace for $3.4 billion in annualized revenue, with potential to scale to $30 billion or more. Despite a 700% stock rise in the past year, analysts suggest significant growth potential remains.

IREN.headline: Why I Just Bought Even More of These 3 Underappreciated AI Stocks
IREN.description: The article highlights three underappreciated AI stocks poised for growth: Silicon Motion Technology (SIMO), which provides NAND flash controllers for memory chips with 46% YoY revenue growth; IREN, a neocloud GPU-as-a-Service provider with a $9.7B Microsoft deal and potential for $40B in annual recurring revenue; and Broadcom (AVGO), a custom AI chipmaker with 47% expected YoY revenue growth in Q2 2026 and a $10B buyback program.

AXTI.headline: This Obscure AI Stock Is Up by Almost 6,000% Over the Past Year. Can It Go Higher?
AXTI.description: AXT, a semiconductor wafer substrate manufacturer, has surged nearly 6,000% in the past year due to its indium phosphide wafers used in AI chips. However, the company's fundamentals don't match its valuation—revenue declined year-over-year, it's still unprofitable, and the stock trades at a stretched 38x price-to-sales ratio. While AXT controls 80-90% of the indium phosphide market and is gaining Tier-1 customers, the current valuation requires perfection to justify, making it a risky investment

ARM.headline: Why Arm Holdings Stock Was Tumbling Today
ARM.description: Arm Holdings stock pulled back after surging on Intel's strong earnings report, as investors took profits following a 50% rally in three weeks. Despite the pullback, the CPU market remains buoyed by growing demand from Agentic AI. Arm's valuation has stretched to a P/E of 130, though its new chip design and $25 billion revenue target by 2031 offer growth potential.

ARM.headline: The $1.75 Trillion Launch: Is SpaceX's IPO a Generational Buy -- or the Ultimate Bubble?
ARM.description: SpaceX is planning a historic $1.75 trillion IPO, making it the largest IPO ever. The article analyzes whether this will be a generational buy or ultimate bubble by comparing it to past mega-IPOs like Meta and Arm (winners) versus Alibaba and Rivian (losers). The author concludes SpaceX shares more similarities with successful IPOs, though the unprecedented valuation makes historical comparisons unreliable.
